mnemonics: BWP: Bounded & Weierstrass Property. 'Every Infinite Team has a Star Player' - In an infinite set within a bounded team (set), you can always pick a star (convergent subsequence).
keyPoints: A metric space has the Bolzano-Weierstrass property if every infinite bounded subset has a limit point.
In Euclidean spaces (R^n), this property is equivalent to the set being closed and bounded (by the Heine-Borel theorem).
This property is a key characterization of sequential compactness in metric spaces.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with the property of being closed and bounded, which is only equivalent to compactness (and thus the B-W property) in R^n, not in general metric spaces.
Forgetting the 'bounded' condition and assuming any infinite set in a compact space has a limit point (it must also be bounded, though in a compact space, all subsets are bounded).
